You are auditing source code for an INDEPENDENT external code review (client: Telus). You have Bash, Read, Grep tools.

STEP 1 — get your file list. Run:
  /opt/conda/bin/python -c "import json; s=json.load(open('/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/.name_shards.json'))[10]; print(chr(10).join(s))"
That prints your assigned slice as lines of "relpath:START-END" (relative to /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent).

STEP 2 — GENUINELY READ every one of your assigned files/ranges, line by line, using the Read tool with the given offset/limit. Do NOT grep-and-skim. Actually read the code so you catch names a keyword search would miss.

STEP 3 — flag EVERY name (module/file name, class, function/method, variable, constant, enum value) that would look UNPROFESSIONAL in an external code review. This includes:
  - marketing / quality adjectives: honest, real, true, smart, magic, ultimate, enhanced, improved, robust, proper(when boastful), aggressive, killer, awesome, blazing, super, mega, best, clean(when boastful)
  - temporal / version markers: v2/v3/v4, r2/r3, _new, _old, legacy, deprecated, next/nextgen, final(when it means 'this version'), fixed, temp, wip, draft, backup, orig, working, experimental, proto
  - jokes / informal / vague: hack, yolo, stuff, misc, foo, doit, thing, blah, magic numbers named as words
Judge at a STRICT bar: **when in doubt, mark RENAME, not KEEP.** Only mark KEEP for names that are unambiguously standard, descriptive, professional vocabulary or a real domain term (e.g. 'finalize', 'proper_noun', 'evict_older_than', a real product name like 'SmartArt' ONLY if the code truly implements it).

STEP 4 — WRITE your findings by APPENDING TAB-separated rows to /tmp/claude-1000/-home-polaris-polaris-project/21e87760-8436-4090-870d-99ef2121882e/scratchpad/name_findings/shard_10.tsv, one row per flagged name:
  VERDICT<TAB>NAME<TAB>relpath:LINE<TAB>KIND<TAB>WHY_it_reads_unprofessional<TAB>SUGGESTED_professional_name
  (VERDICT = RENAME or KEEP-BUT-NOTED; KIND = file/class/func/var/const/enum)
Use a bash heredoc or printf with real tab characters. If a file range has NO issues, still write one row: NONE<TAB>-<TAB><relpath><TAB>-<TAB>clean<TAB>-.

Return ONLY a one-line summary: "shard 10: <R> renames, <K> kept-noted across <F> files".

- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""I-beatboth-011 #4 + #7 (#1289) — behavioral replay harness for the weighted-
3	enrichment sanitize + same-work consolidate fix.
4	
5	§-1.4 acceptance gate: this proves the EFFECT FIRES in the REAL output of the REAL
6	``build_verified_span_draft`` function (not a reimplementation, not a config check):
7	
8	  (a) #4 RENDER SAFETY: a span carrying a literal NUL byte + a ~75K raw-metadata blob,
9	      after the real draft build, emits ZERO NUL/C0 control bytes and the oversized
10	      blob is screened/bounded out (never reaches the render).
11	  (b) #7 SAME-WORK CONSOLIDATION: 4 members that are the SAME WORK (same DOI,
12	      different URLs) collapse to ONE multi-citation unit-set that STILL retains all 4
13	      URLs as grounding corroborator markers (multi-URL corroboration, ONE source).
14	  (b2) #4 OVER-MERGE NEGATIVE CONTROL: two records with the SAME folded title but
15	      DIFFERENT year AND different first author (no DOI) are DIFFERENT works and must
16	      NOT consolidate — each surfaces as its OWN distinct single-marker unit.
17	  (c) #7 CAPTCHA DROP: a member whose fetched text is a CAPTCHA / security stub
18	      ("Just a moment... Performing security verification") is dropped entirely.
19	
20	FAIL LOUD: sys.exit(1) with a diagnostic on ANY regression; sys.exit(0) only if all
21	three checks pass. No network, no LLM, no faithfulness-gate touch — pure offline replay.
22	"""
23	
24	from __future__ import annotations
25	
26	import os
27	import re
28	import sys
29	
30	# Ensure repo root on path when run from anywhere.
31	_REPO_ROOT = os.path.dirname(os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__)))
32	if _REPO_ROOT not in sys.path:
33	    sys.path.insert(0, _REPO_ROOT)
34	
35	from src.polaris_graph.generator.weighted_enrichment import (  # noqa: E402
36	    build_verified_span_draft,
37	)
38	
39	# Legacy [ev_id] marker form the production rewriter consumes (live_deepseek_generator.py:64).
40	_EV_MARKER_RE = re.compile(r"\[([A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*)\]")
41	
42	_FAILURES: list[str] = []
43	
44	
45	def _fail(check: str, msg: str) -> None:
46	    _FAILURES.append(f"[{check}] {msg}")
47	
48	
49	# A clean clinical claim sentence used as the shared verbatim quote for the same-work
50	# group. Long enough to clear _MIN_UNIT_CHARS (40) and short enough to clear the render
51	# char bound (default 600).
52	_CLEAN_SENTENCE = (
53	    "Automation displaced roughly 12 percent of routine clerical tasks across the "
54	    "surveyed labor markets between 2020 and 2024"
55	)
56	
57	
58	def _check_a_render_safety() -> None:
59	    """#4: NUL + 75K blob span must never reach the render."""
60	    blob = "x9 = sum over j of qj " * 4000  # ~88K chars of raw-extraction salad
61	    assert len(blob) > 75_000, "test blob must exceed 75K chars"
62	    # The clean sentence ITSELF carries an embedded NUL + C0/DEL bytes, so this check
63	    # exercises the control-byte STRIP on an IN-BOUND, surviving unit (not only the
64	    # char-bound drop of the blob) — the strip is independently load-bearing.
65	    clean_with_ctrl = (
66	        "Automation displaced roughly 12\x00 percent of routine\x01 clerical tasks "
67	        "across the surveyed\x7f labor markets between 2020 and 2024"
68	    )
69	    poisoned_quote = (
70	        f"{clean_with_ctrl}.\nKD = sum_j Rk \x00 wj(h) \x00 Rk equilibrium dump "
71	        f"{blob}\x01\x02end."
72	    )
73	    pool = {
74	        "ev_poison": {
75	            "evidence_id": "ev_poison",
76	            "direct_quote": poisoned_quote,
77	            "doi": "10.1234/poison",
78	            "title": "Equilibrium properties of automated labor markets",
79	            "source_url": "https://example.org/poison",
80	        },
81	    }
82	    draft = build_verified_span_draft(["ev_poison"], pool)
83	
84	    if "\x00" in draft:
85	        _fail("a", "draft STILL contains a NUL byte (\\x00) — binary corruption not fixed")
86	    for cp in range(0x20):
87	        ch = chr(cp)
88	        if ch in ("\n", "\t"):
89	            continue
90	        if ch in draft:
91	            _fail("a", f"draft contains C0 control byte U+{cp:04X} — control bytes not stripped")
92	    if "\x7f" in draft:
93	        _fail("a", "draft contains DEL (0x7F) — control bytes not stripped")
94	    # The clean sentence (under the bound) SHOULD still surface; the blob must NOT.
95	    if "Automation displaced roughly 12 percent" not in draft:
96	        _fail("a", "the clean in-bound clinical sentence was lost (over-screening)")
97	    if "sum over j of qj" in draft or len(draft) > 5_000:
98	        _fail(
99	            "a",
100	            f"the oversized raw-extraction blob leaked into the render (draft_chars={len(draft)})",
101	        )
102	
103	
104	def _check_b_same_work_consolidation() -> None:
105	    """#7: 4 same-work members (same DOI, 4 URLs) collapse to ONE multi-citation unit."""
106	    quote = f"{_CLEAN_SENTENCE}."
107	    shared_doi = "10.1000/labor.2024.001"
108	    member_ids = ["ev_w1", "ev_w2", "ev_w3", "ev_w4"]
109	    urls = [
110	        "https://journal.example.org/full",
111	        "https://mirror.example.net/pdf",
112	        "https://repo.example.edu/preprint",
113	        "https://aggregator.example.com/view",
114	    ]
115	    pool = {
116	        eid: {
117	            "evidence_id": eid,
118	            # SAME verbatim quote on each fetch (multi-URL corroboration of one work).
119	            "direct_quote": quote,
120	            "doi": shared_doi,
121	            "title": "Automation and the clerical labor market",
122	            "source_url": url,
123	        }
124	        for eid, url in zip(member_ids, urls)
125	    }
126	    # Per-source budget of 1 so we deterministically get exactly one unit for the work.
127	    os.environ["PG_BREADTH_ENRICHMENT_SPANS_PER_SOURCE"] = "1"
128	    try:
129	        draft = build_verified_span_draft(member_ids, pool)
130	    finally:

132

134	    occurrences = draft.count("Automation displaced roughly 12 percent")
135	    if occurrences != 1:
136	        _fail(
137	            "b",

312	def _check_c_captcha_stub_dropped() -> None:
313	    """#7: a CAPTCHA / security-stub member is dropped; a clean member survives.
314	
315	    I-beatboth-011 #7 P1 (#1289): the drop requires the trigger phrase "just a moment"
316	    AND a strong WAF/security co-token. A bare "just a moment" in otherwise-substantive
317	    prose must NOT be dropped (§-1.3 keep-all).
318	
319	    POSITIVE: "Just a moment... Performing security verification... Cloudflare Ray ID"
320	    carries trigger + co-tokens => DROPPED.
321	    NEGATIVE CONTROL: "Just a moment, the unemployment rate fell to 3.5% in 2023 according
322	    to BLS" carries the bare trigger but NO security co-token => real prose => KEPT.
323	    """
324	    bare_sentence = (
325	        "Just a moment, the unemployment rate fell to 3.5 percent in 2023 according to "
326	        "the Bureau of Labor Statistics surveyed establishment data"
327	    )
328	    pool = {
329	        "ev_captcha": {
330	            "evidence_id": "ev_captcha",
331	            "direct_quote": (
332	                "Just a moment... www.example.org needs to review the security of your "
333	                "connection before proceeding. Performing security verification. "
334	                "Cloudflare Ray ID: abc123."
335	            ),
336	            "doi": "10.9999/captcha",
337	            "title": "Cloudflare interstitial stub page",

339	        },

354	    }
355	    draft = build_verified_span_draft(["ev_captcha", "ev_bare", "ev_clean"], pool)
356	    markers = _EV_MARKER_RE.findall(draft)
357	    if "ev_captcha" in markers:
358	        _fail("c", "CAPTCHA-stub member ev_captcha was NOT dropped (its marker leaked)")
359	    if "security verification" in draft.lower() or "cloudflare ray id" in draft.lower():

361	    if "ev_bare" not in markers:

364	            "NEGATIVE CONTROL: bare 'just a moment' real prose (no security co-token) was "

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""I-beatboth-011 idx49 (#1289) — behavioral replay-harness for the fetch-shell
3	EVIDENCE-PATH leak (§-1.4 fail-loud).
4	
5	THE DEFECT this proves the fix for: ``clean_fetch_body`` returns
6	``CleanedFetch(cleaned_text, shell_reason)``. ``shell_reason`` is set (non-None)
7	when the WHOLE cleaned body is a fetch SHELL (boilerplate / soft-404 / a wholly
8	chrome reference-listing page that cleans to empty). The FRAME-ENTITY path already
9	consumes this signal and routes a shell to its METADATA_ONLY gap branch. But the
10	two EVIDENCE-path consumers in ``live_retriever`` discarded ``shell_reason``
11	(``clean_fetch_body(content).cleaned_text``) and built a cited ``direct_quote`` /
12	evidence row from the junk anyway. On the drb_72 run this leaked junk (e.g. the
13	scirp.org reference-listing page below) into the cited evidence pool.
14	
15	THE FIX consumes the EXISTING ``shell_reason`` signal at BOTH evidence-path sites
16	(``refetch_for_extraction_with_diagnostics`` ~L2078 and the ``run_live_retrieval``
17	per-URL row builder ~L4561) and routes a shell row to the EXISTING skip/gap branch
18	(NOT a cited evidence row). No new drop/cap/threshold; the existing 1200-char gate
19	is untouched; the faithfulness engine is untouched.
20	
21	This is a BEHAVIORAL end-to-end harness (NOT a flag-set / unit-of-clean_fetch_body
22	check). It drives the REAL consumers and asserts on the EMITTED evidence:
23	  • Case A (EXCLUDED): a wholly-junk shell body is NOT emitted as a cited evidence
24	    row / direct_quote — it is routed to the existing skip/gap branch.
25	  • Case B (KEPT): a real article body IS emitted as a normal evidence row — the
26	    fix never drops a real source (§-1.3 removes only confirmed fetch-junk).
27	
28	Both fixture bodies are ADAPTED from REAL bodies in the drb_72 evidence pool
29	(``outputs/audits/drb_72_2026_06_12/runA_final/evidence_pool.json``) — same source,
30	same shape, lightly trimmed/representative (not byte-verbatim):
31	  • JUNK  = a scirp.org reference-listing page (all markdown link-chrome) that
32	            ``clean_fetch_body`` cleans to empty → ``shell_reason='empty_after_clean'``.
33	            The stored scirp quote in that pool re-cleans to empty under the current
34	            chrome-stripper; this is the genuine fetch-junk the SITE-2 fix removes.
35	  • REAL  = the AEA ``jep.33.2.3`` automation/labor article body → ``shell_reason=None``.
36	
37	SCOPE — what this fix does and does NOT close (honest, per §-1.1):
38	  • Closes the EMPTY-after-clean / boilerplate-dominated error-stub shell class at
39	    BOTH evidence-path sites (e.g. the scirp reference-listing page).
40	  • Does NOT close the longer Cloudflare/CAPTCHA INTERSTITIAL class named in the
41	    issue — the IMF elibrary "solve a puzzle" and BMJ "you have been blocked" bodies
42	    (~500-780 chars) fire neither ``clean_fetch_body.shell_reason`` NOR frame_fetcher's
43	    ``_is_fetch_shell`` and REMAIN cited. Catching that class needs a NEW phrase
44	    detector, which is a NEW drop rule (forbidden here by §-1.3 / the no-new-threshold
45	    constraint) → tracked as a separate follow-up, NOT this PR.
46	  • Site-1 note: for the empty-cleaning scirp fixture, site 1 ALREADY excluded it
47	    pre-fix via the ``thin_content`` length gate; the idx49 fix re-labels that
48	    exclusion ``fetch_shell`` (honest attribution). The genuine NEW exclusion is at
49	    site 2, whose per-URL row builder has no length gate before the append.
50	
51	FAIL-LOUD: any assertion failure exits non-zero. PASS exits 0.
52	
53	Run:  python scripts/iarch_beatboth011_idx49_fetchshell_evidence_harness.py
54	"""

61	_REPO_ROOT = os.path.dirname(os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__)))
62	if _REPO_ROOT not in sys.path:

70	#
71	# JUNK: a scirp.org "reference list" page (its ABSTRACT tail trimmed for the
72	# fixture). Every line is markdown link-chrome (TITLE/AUTHORS/KEYWORDS/JOURNAL
73	# NAME all point at ``../journal/...`` slugs). ``strip_web_boilerplate`` strips
74	# the link-chrome and the whole unit cleans to empty → ``clean_fetch_body``
75	# returns ``shell_reason='empty_after_clean'``. On the RAW body it is NOT content-
76	# starved and NOT a landing page, so at SITE 2 (the per-URL row builder, which has
77	# no length gate before the append) NO prior gate excludes it — only the idx49 fix
78	# does. The stored scirp quote in the drb_72 pool re-cleans to empty the same way.

81	    "Future Skills Set](../journal/paperinformation?paperid=95586) AUTHORS: "

83	    "authors&page=1), [Michael J. Kendzia](../journal/articles?searchcode="
84	    "Michael+J.+Kendzia&searchfield=authors&page=1) KEYWORDS: [Destruction Effect]"
85	    "(../journal/articles?searchcode=Destruction+Effect&searchfield=keyword&page=1), "
86	    "[Capitalization Effect](../journal/articles?searchcode=Capitalization+Effect&"
87	    "searchfield=keyword&page=1), [Soft Skills](../journal/articles?searchcode="
88	    "Soft+Skills&searchfield=keyword&page=1) JOURNAL NAME: [Advances in Applied "
89	    "Sociology](../journal/home?journalid=1002), [Vol.9 No.10](../journal/home?"

94	# automation/labor article (jep.33.2.3) — its abstract head, lightly extended for
95	# the fixture so it reads as a full body. Long real prose; ``clean_fetch_body``
96	# leaves it intact with ``shell_reason=None``. This row MUST survive the fix as a

151	def _stub_fetch_content_factory(body: str):
152	    """Return a ``_fetch_content`` stub yielding ``body`` for every URL.
153	
154	    Mirrors the real ``_fetch_content`` 5-tuple
155	    ``(content, ok, title, body_type, jsonld)``. ``ok=True`` + a non-empty body

158	    NOT use the degraded ``ok=False`` path so the ONLY thing that can exclude the

168	def _run_seed_only(monkeypatch_env, *, body: str, seed_url: str) -> list:
169	    """Drive the REAL ``run_live_retrieval`` seed_only loop on one URL whose
170	    fetched body is ``body``. Returns the emitted ``evidence_rows``.
171	
172	    The redesign flag is ON — that is the cert config where the junk leaked, and
173	    it is the ONLY config under which a row reaches the down-weight-and-KEEP
174	    branch (so the idx49 guard must pre-empt it). Offline + cheap: OA resolver
175	    off, OpenAlex enrichment stubbed, serial fetch.
176	    """
177	    saved = {}
178	    for k, v in monkeypatch_env.items():
179	        saved[k] = os.environ.get(k)
180	        os.environ[k] = v
181	
182	    orig_fetch = lr._fetch_content
183	    orig_enrich = getattr(lr, "_bounded_openalex_enrich", None)
184	    try:

186	        if orig_enrich is not None:

188	        result = lr.run_live_retrieval(

197	    finally:

199	        if orig_enrich is not None:
200	            lr._bounded_openalex_enrich = orig_enrich
201	        for k, old in saved.items():
202	            if old is None:
203	                os.environ.pop(k, None)
204	            else:
205	                os.environ[k] = old
206	
207	
208	_ENV = {

210	    "PG_USE_PARALLEL_FETCH": "0",
211	    # Redesign ON: the cert config where the junk leaked + the only path that
212	    # reaches the down-weight-and-keep branch the guard must pre-empt.
213	    "PG_SWEEP_CREDIBILITY_REDESIGN": "1",
214	    # Keep the loop offline + cheap.
215	    "PG_ENABLE_LIVE_OA_RESOLVER": "0",
216	}
217	
218

227	            f"{[r.get('source_url') for r in rows]!r} — the idx49 guard did not "
228	            "fire on the real-output path"
229	        )

231	
232	
233	def test_site2_real_article_still_emitted() -> None:

235	    populated direct_quote. The fix must never drop a real source."""

237	    if len(rows) != 1:
238	        _fail(

240	            f"(got {len(rows)} rows) — the fix dropped a real source"
241	        )
242	    row = rows[0]
243	    if not row.get("direct_quote") or len(row["direct_quote"]) < 100:
244	        _fail(
245	            "real article evidence row has no usable direct_quote "
246	            f"(len={len(row.get('direct_quote', ''))})"
247	        )
248	    if "displacement effect" not in row["direct_quote"].lower():
249	        _fail("real article direct_quote did not carry the source prose")
250	    print("  [site2] PASS: real article emitted as a normal cited evidence row")

252	
253	def test_site1_junk_shell_not_extractable() -> None:
254	    """Case A for site 1 — ``refetch_for_extraction_with_diagnostics`` returns an

257	    orig_fetch = lr._fetch_content
258	    try:
259	        lr._fetch_content = _stub_fetch_content_factory(_JUNK_SHELL_BODY)
260	        quote, diag = lr.refetch_for_extraction_with_diagnostics(
261	            "https://www.scirp.org/reference/referencespapers?referenceid=2601244"
262	        )

267	            "site1: junk fetch-shell produced a NON-empty extraction quote "
268	            f"(len={len(quote)}) — it would be cited as evidence"
269	        )
270	    if diag.get("failure_mode") != "fetch_shell":
271	        _fail(
272	            "site1: junk fetch-shell did not record failure_mode='fetch_shell' "
273	            f"(got {diag.get('failure_mode')!r})"
274	        )
275	    print("  [site1] PASS: junk fetch-shell -> empty quote, failure_mode='fetch_shell'")
276	
277	
278	def test_site1_real_article_extractable() -> None:
279	    """Case B for site 1 — a real article body still yields a non-empty extraction
280	    quote (eligible). The fix never drops a real source."""
281	    orig_fetch = lr._fetch_content

283	        lr._fetch_content = _stub_fetch_content_factory(_REAL_ARTICLE_BODY)
284	        quote, diag = lr.refetch_for_extraction_with_diagnostics(

290	        _fail(
291	            "site1: real article did not yield a usable extraction quote "
292	            f"(len={len(quote)}) — the fix dropped a real source"
293	        )
294	    if diag.get("failure_mode"):
295	        _fail(
296	            "site1: real article recorded a failure_mode "
297	            f"({diag.get('failure_mode')!r}) — it must be eligible"
298	        )
299	    print("  [site1] PASS: real article -> non-empty extraction quote, eligible")
300	
301	
302	def main() -> int:
303	    print("I-beatboth-011 idx49 — fetch-shell evidence-path behavioral harness")
304	    print("Fixtures: REAL drb_72 bodies (scirp.org junk shell + AEA jep.33.2.3 article)")
305	    print()

309	    test_site2_junk_shell_excluded_from_cited_evidence()

2	"""I-beatboth-011 (#1289) — fail-loud harness for the junk-SOURCE screen.
3	
4	§-1.4 behavioral acceptance (non-zero exit on regression). The defect: a handful
5	of NON-source pages were cited / listed in the bibliography + per-claim
6	corroboration of a freshly-rendered wide report
7	(outputs/p6_fixed_replay/workforce/drb_72_ai_labor/report.md, 526 cited sources):
8	  (a) a Chegg homework page ("Solved Please read the following paper: David H.
9	      Autor ... | Chegg.com") stood in for a real paper — [521] in the bibliography
10	      AND woven into the body prose; and
11	  (b) a JS-error-shell page ("We're sorry but our[site] doesn't work properly
12	      without JavaScript enabled. Please enable it to continue.") was quoted as a
13	      corroborator across many claims (host = openalex.org, a REAL repository,
14	      whose other rows carry real abstracts that MUST be kept).
15	
16	FIX: a high-precision, faithfulness-NEUTRAL junk-SOURCE screen
17	(``access_bypass.is_junk_source`` = ``is_junk_source_host`` OR
18	``is_error_shell_text``) wired at the single corpus-consumption seam in
19	``run_honest_sweep_r3.py`` (after every retrieval/merge lane AND the fresh/resume
20	reconcile, BEFORE selection / the approval gate). Junk = a homework-help /
21	Q&A-not-source HOST or a fetch-error SHELL body. It drops ONLY junk — never a
22	real journal / repository / gov / news source (§-1.3 keep-all for REAL sources).
23	strict_verify / NLI / 4-role / span-grounding are untouched.
24	
25	Asserts (each FAILS LOUD with non-zero exit on regression):
26	  (A) PREDICATE PRECISION:
27	        - a Chegg URL source is DROPPED (host signal);
28	        - an error-shell ("doesn't work properly without JavaScript") source is
29	          DROPPED (text signal);
30	        - a real journal / doi.org / pubmed / repository source is KEPT;
31	        - a real news source is KEPT;
32	        - near-miss hosts ("notchegg.com") and real long prose that merely QUOTES
33	          an error phrase are KEPT (no over-screen).
34	  (B) BEHAVIORAL on the REAL banked corpus_snapshot: when the screen is applied to
35	      the actual drb_72 corpus, EVERY Chegg + JS-error-shell row is removed from
36	      retrieval.evidence_rows + classified_sources, while the REAL openalex.org
37	      abstract rows on the SAME host SURVIVE (proves the effect fires in the real
38	      consumption path, not just the predicate — §-1.4).
39	"""

54	# The exact banked junk strings (drb_72 corpus_snapshot, p6_fixed_replay).
55	_CHEGG_URL = (

57	    "please-read-following-paper-david-h-autor-still-many-jobs-history-"
58	    "future-workplace-automat-q105413568"

61	    "We're sorry but ourresearch-website-2 doesn't work properly without "
62	    "JavaScript enabled. Please enable it to continue."

65	_REAL_OPENALEX_BODIES = (
66	    "Don't Fear the Robots: Automatability and Job Satisfaction DetailsLocations "
67	    "Year: 2020 Type: preprint Abstract: We analyze automatability and job "
68	    "satisfaction across occupations using survey microdata, finding "
69	    "heterogeneous effects on worker wellbeing.",
70	    "Job Polarization and Structural Change Year: 2014 Type: preprint Abstract: "
71	    "Job polarization is a widely documented phenomenon in advanced economies "
72	    "driven by routine-biased technical change and offshoring.",

76	def _check_predicate() -> None:

78	        is_error_shell_text,

80	        is_junk_source_host,

94	    # path (which is gated by the same residual<=3-words precision guard, so a

101	        _fail("genuine WAF/cloudflare block interstitial not flagged (co-token path)")

104	    real_hosts = (
105	        "https://doi.org/10.48550/arxiv.2306.15895",
106	        "https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/12345678/",
107	        "https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC9334306/",
108	        "https://www.nature.com/articles/s41586-024-00001-x",
109	        "https://arxiv.org/abs/2306.15895",
110	        "https://openalex.org/W4400432169",
111	        "https://www.oecd.org/en/publications/ai-and-the-future-of-skills.html",
112	        "https://www.bls.gov/news.release/empsit.nr0.htm",

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""I-beatboth-011 keystone-F1 (#1284) — behavioral replay harness for WITHIN-BASKET multi-cited
3	verified synthesis + the relational-quantifier guard.
4	
5	§-1.4 fail-loud (non-zero exit) acceptance. The keystone-F1 effect must ACTUALLY FIRE in the composed
6	output, not merely pass a unit test. The shape proven here is the WITHIN-BASKET, per-member synthesis
7	(one CLAIM, N corroborating SUPPORTS members of the SAME basket co-located into ONE multi-cited
8	sentence) — distinct from the I-beatboth-002 cross-basket producer. This is the §-1.3 DNA reading of
9	"corroboration": repetition across the basket's members is STRENGTH, surfaced as multiple citations on
10	one sentence, never collapsed to the single strongest member.
11	
12	Three assertions (the whole point — prove it WIRED, not just present):
13	
14	  (i)   MULTI-CITATION FIRES — a basket with >=2 corroborating verified SUPPORTS members yields ONE
15	        multi-cited sentence carrying ALL of their [#ev:...] tokens, and that sentence strict_verify-
16	        PASSES per-clause against the UNCHANGED production verifier (every clause keeps its own span).
17	  (ii)  THE GUARD DROPS A FABRICATED RELATIONAL QUANTIFIER — a candidate multi-cited sentence whose
18	        writer prepended an aggregate predicate ("most studies show ...") that the basket consensus
19	        state does NOT license is REJECTED/repaired by relational_quantifier_guard, so the fabricated
20	        quantifier never reaches the rendered sentence. (under-relax is safe; an unverifiable
21	        quantifier is the lethal direction.)
22	  (iii) SINGLE-SOURCE PATH IS BYTE-IDENTICAL — a basket with exactly ONE verified member produces the
23	        unchanged single-cite sentence (the multi-cited producer adds breadth, it never rewrites the
24	        one-source path).
25	
26	RED BASELINE: run against the UNMODIFIED tree (no ``_join_verified_clauses`` / no
27	``relational_quantifier_guard``) — the import guard FAILS LOUD with a non-zero exit. After keystone-F1
28	lands it PASSES. "Committed + green + Codex-approve" is NOT acceptance; this firing-in-output gate is.
29	
30	REAL CORPUS: loads a real banked ``corpus_snapshot.json`` (the real evidence pool + real evidence_ids)
31	and binds each real id to a controlled verified span, so strict_verify is deterministic — no network,
32	no model spend, no relaxation. The LLM writer is FAKE/injected; ``strict_verify`` is the REAL
33	production single-sentence verifier (``verify_sentence_provenance``).
34	"""
35	from __future__ import annotations

63	# and portable. These are THREE corroborating phrasings of the SAME claim (productivity gains from

65	_SPAN_1 = "Generative AI adoption raised measured worker productivity in customer-support roles."

83	def _load_real_evidence_ids(n: int) -> list[str]:
84	    """Load the REAL corpus_snapshot and return the first ``n`` distinct real evidence_ids. FAIL LOUD
85	    if the snapshot is missing / malformed / too small — the harness must run on real banked data."""

132	    citing that member's OWN id+span. The only injected part; strict_verify is real. Returns the

324	              f"multi-citation must FIRE with DIFFERENT decimals per clause (no whole-sentence decimal "
325	              f"check degrading it to one K-span); cited={sorted(dec_ids)} sentence={dec!r}")
326	    if "12" not in dec or "8" not in dec:
327	        _fail("iv_decimal_lost", f"both clauses' decimals must survive in the joined sentence; got {dec!r}")
328	    dgv = verify_sentence_provenance(dec, evidence_pool)
329	    if not bool(getattr(dgv, "is_verified", False)):
330	        _fail("iv_decimal_not_verified",
331	              f"the decimal-bearing joined sentence must strict_verify PER CLAUSE; rejected: {dec!r}")
332	
333	    # ── (v) A VERBATIM SOURCE QUANTIFIER IS PRESERVED (advisor gap #1 — faithfulness). When a member's
334	    #    writer clause FAILS verify, the producer falls back to that member's VERBATIM K-span. The source's
335	    #    OWN word "Most ..." is a quotation, NOT a fabricated aggregate — the guard must NEVER delete it.
336	    #    A writer that returns nothing forces the verbatim K-span path for both members. ──
337	    vq_pool = dict(evidence_pool)
338	    vq_pool[eid_3] = {"evidence_id": eid_3, "direct_quote": _SPAN_VERBATIM_Q}
339	    vq_basket = _basket(
340	        "b_verbatim_q", "firm productivity",
341	        [_member(eid_3, _SPAN_VERBATIM_Q, "ovq"), _member(eid_solo, _SPAN_SOLO, "ovq2")],
342	    )
343	    def _empty_writer(_b, _p) -> str:  # forces the verbatim K-span fallback for every member
344	        return ""
345	    vq = compose_basket_multicited_sentence(
346	        vq_basket, vq_pool, writer_fn=_empty_writer, verify_fn=verify_sentence_provenance,
347	    )
348	    if not vq or not vq.strip():
349	        _fail("v_verbatim_absent", "the verbatim-quantifier basket produced no multi-cited sentence.")
350	    # The source's own "Most of the surveyed firms ..." MUST appear verbatim (the guard left it alone).
351	    if "most of the surveyed firms" not in vq.lower():
352	        _fail("v_verbatim_quantifier_mutated",
353	              f"the guard MUST NOT delete a quantifier the SOURCE itself wrote (verbatim K-span "
354	              f"misquoted); got {vq!r}")
355	    vqgv = verify_sentence_provenance(vq, vq_pool)
356	    if not bool(getattr(vqgv, "is_verified", False)):
357	        _fail("v_verbatim_not_verified",
358	              f"the verbatim-K-span multi-cited sentence must strict_verify; rejected: {vq!r}")
359	
360	    # ── (v-b) THE REAL DEFAULT WRITER preserves a verbatim source quantifier (advisor gap #1, deeper).
361	    #    The DEFAULT production writer is build_short_member_sentence — it returns the source's VERBATIM
362	    #    first sentence, which PASSES verify, so it flows through the WRITER path (not the K-span fallback).
363	    #    The guard must NOT strip "Most of the surveyed firms ..." from that VERBATIM writer output either —
364	    #    a quantifier PRESENT IN THE MEMBER'S SPAN is the source's word, never a fabrication. (Case (v) used
365	    #    an empty writer; this case exercises the real writer where the misquote actually bites.) ──
366	    from src.polaris_graph.generator.verified_compose import build_short_member_sentence  # noqa: PLC0415
367	    _short_writer = lambda _b, _p: build_short_member_sentence(_b, vq_pool)  # the actual default writer_fn
368	    vq2 = compose_basket_multicited_sentence(
369	        vq_basket, vq_pool, writer_fn=_short_writer, verify_fn=verify_sentence_provenance,
370	    )
371	    if not vq2 or not vq2.strip():
372	        _fail("vb_real_writer_absent", "the verbatim-quantifier basket produced nothing under the real writer.")
373	    if "most of the surveyed firms" not in vq2.lower():
374	        _fail("vb_real_writer_quantifier_mutated",
375	              f"the guard MUST NOT delete a quantifier the SOURCE wrote even when it arrives via the REAL "
376	              f"writer (build_short_member_sentence returns the verbatim span); misquoted: {vq2!r}")
377	    vq2gv = verify_sentence_provenance(vq2, vq_pool)
378	    if not bool(getattr(vq2gv, "is_verified", False)):
379	        _fail("vb_real_writer_not_verified",
380	              f"the real-writer verbatim-quantifier sentence must strict_verify; rejected: {vq2!r}")
381	
382	    # ── (vi) ABSTRACTIVE-WRITER KEYING (advisor gap #2). The real abstractive writer is a precomputed
383	    #    dict keyed by the basket's claim_cluster_id and precomputes ONE WHOLE-basket draft per basket.
384	    #    The within-basket producer must STILL fire multi-cite under that writer — the per-member sub-basket
385	    #    lookup returns the parent's whole-basket draft (or misses), which fails the single-member scoped
386	    #    pool, so each member cleanly falls back to its OWN verbatim K-span. Mimic the real keying here. ──
387	    from src.polaris_graph.generator.abstractive_writer import (  # noqa: PLC0415
388	        make_abstractive_writer_fn,
389	    )
390	    # A precomputed dict keyed by claim_cluster_id, holding a whole-basket synthesized draft (as the real
391	    # pre-pass produces) — cites a DIFFERENT member's id than the sub-basket scopes to, so verify fails on
392	    # the single-member pool and the member falls back to its verbatim K-span (proving multi-cite still
393	    # fires from the deterministic spans regardless of the writer path).
394	    whole_basket_draft = f"AI adoption raised productivity across the firms [#ev:{eid_1}:0-{len(_SPAN_1)}]."
395	    precomputed = {"b_corroborated": whole_basket_draft}
396	    aw_writer = make_abstractive_writer_fn(precomputed)
397	    aw = compose_basket_multicited_sentence(
398	        multi_basket, evidence_pool, writer_fn=aw_writer, verify_fn=verify_sentence_provenance,
399	    )
400	    if not aw or not aw.strip():
401	        _fail("vi_abstractive_absent",
402	              "multi-citation did NOT fire under the real abstractive-writer keying (silent no-op).")
403	    aw_ids = {str(getattr(t, "evidence_id", "") or "") for t in parse_provenance_tokens(aw)}
404	    if not ({eid_1, eid_2, eid_3} <= aw_ids):
405	        _fail("vi_abstractive_degraded",
406	              f"under the claim_cluster_id-keyed writer the producer must STILL surface all THREE members "
407	              f"(via per-member verbatim K-span fallback), not degrade to one K-span; cited={sorted(aw_ids)} "
408	              f"sentence={aw!r}")
409	    awgv = verify_sentence_provenance(aw, evidence_pool)
410	    if not bool(getattr(awgv, "is_verified", False)):
411	        _fail("vi_abstractive_not_verified",
412	              f"the abstractive-writer-path multi-cited sentence must strict_verify; rejected: {aw!r}")
413	
414	    print("PASS iarch-beatboth-011 keystone-F1 within-basket multi-cited synthesis harness "
415	          f"(real corpus: {_CORPUS_SNAPSHOT.name}, real ids: {eid_1}/{eid_2}/{eid_3}/{eid_solo}/"
416	          f"{eid_d1}/{eid_d2}): "
417	          "(i) multi-citation FIRES — a 3-member corroborated basket renders ONE sentence carrying ALL "
418	          "THREE [#ev] tokens, every clause strict_verify-PASSES; "
419	          "(ii) the relational-quantifier guard DROPS a fabricated 'most studies show ...' that the "
420	          "basket consensus does not license (direct + end-to-end through the producer), residue still "
421	          "strict_verifies; "
422	          "(iii) a single-source basket produces the UNCHANGED single-cite sentence (byte-identical); "
423	          "(iv) a DECIMAL-bearing 2-member basket fires multi-cite + verifies PER CLAUSE (12% and 8% both "
424	          "survive); "
425	          "(v) a quantifier the SOURCE itself wrote ('Most of the surveyed firms ...') is PRESERVED "
426	          "verbatim (the guard never mutates a verbatim K-span); "
427	          "(vi) under the REAL abstractive-writer keying (claim_cluster_id, whole-basket draft) multi-cite "
428	          "STILL fires from per-member verbatim K-spans (no silent degrade). "
429	          "strict_verify untouched; no network, no model spend.")
430	    return 0
431	
432	
433	if __name__ == "__main__":
434	    sys.exit(main())

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""I-beatboth-011 idx 15 (#1289) — fail-loud harness for the pack_drb2 id<->idx + topic-overlap guards.
3	
4	§-1.4 behavioral acceptance (non-zero exit on regression). The defect: a DRB-II run's internal id
5	("task72") does NOT equal its catalog `idx` field (task72 -> idx 56), and the old `task_id == idx`
6	guard PASSES when both are wrong-but-equal — so packing AI-labor prose with `--idx 72` silently
7	scored it against idx-72's task (Parkinson's), a near-zero (§-1.4 silently-wrong-number).
8	
9	Asserts (against the REAL third_party/DeepResearch-Bench-II/tasks_and_rubrics.jsonl):
10	  (1) CORRECT idx (56, the AI-labor task) packs cleanly — no SystemExit.
11	  (2) WRONG idx (72, Parkinson's) raises SystemExit (the topic-overlap guard fires on zero overlap).
12	  (3) id<->idx offset: --task-name "task72" with --idx 72 raises SystemExit (task72 resolves to idx 56).
13	  (4) id<->idx offset: --task-name "task72" with --idx 56 packs cleanly (correct pairing).
14	"""
15	from __future__ import annotations
16	
17	import sys
18	import tempfile
19	from pathlib import Path
20	
21	_REPO = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
22	sys.path.insert(0, str(_REPO))
23	
24	from scripts.dr_benchmark import pack_drb2  # noqa: E402
25	
26	_TASKS = pack_drb2._DEFAULT_TASKS_JSONL
27	
28	# A realistic AI-labor report (matches DRB-II idx 56 "Impact of Generative AI on the Labor Market").
29	_AI_LABOR_REPORT = """# Generative AI and the Labor Market: Displacement, Wages, and Reinstatement
30	
31	## Executive Summary
32	Generative artificial intelligence has measurably affected employment, wages, and occupational
33	exposure across the labor market. Field experiments show customer-support productivity gains, while
34	the task framework predicts both displacement and reinstatement effects on workers.
35	
36	## Bibliography
37	[1] Example.
38	"""
39	
40	
41	def _fail(msg: str) -> None:
42	    print(f"FAIL I-beatboth-011 idx15 pack_drb2 guard: {msg}")
43	    sys.exit(1)
44	
45	
46	def _pack(idx: int, *, task_name: str | None, out_dir: Path):
47	    out_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
48	    report = out_dir / "report.md"
49	    report.write_text(_AI_LABOR_REPORT, encoding="utf-8")
50	    return pack_drb2.pack_one(
51	        report, idx, task_name=task_name, tasks_jsonl=_TASKS,
52	        out_dir=out_dir / "out", model="polaris", strict_truncation=True,
53	    )
54	
55	
56	def main() -> None:
57	    if not _TASKS.exists():
58	        _fail(f"DRB-II catalog not found at {_TASKS} — cannot exercise the guard (needed for this harness)")
59	
60	    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as td:
61	        td = Path(td)
62	
63	        # (1) correct idx 56 (AI-labor) -> clean pack.
64	        try:
65	            out = _pack(56, task_name=None, out_dir=td / "c1")
66	        except SystemExit as e:
67	            _fail(f"(1) correct --idx 56 wrongly raised SystemExit: {e}")
68	        if not out.exists():
69	            _fail("(1) correct --idx 56 did not write the packed file")
70	        print("(1) ok: correct idx 56 (AI-labor) packs cleanly.")
71	
72	        # (2) wrong idx 72 (Parkinson's) -> topic-overlap guard fires.
73	        raised = False
74	        try:
75	            _pack(72, task_name=None, out_dir=td / "c2")
76	        except SystemExit as e:
77	            raised = "topic mismatch" in str(e).lower() or "zero content" in str(e).lower()
78	        if not raised:
79	            _fail("(2) wrong --idx 72 (Parkinson's) did NOT raise the topic-mismatch SystemExit")
80	        print("(2) ok: wrong idx 72 (Parkinson's) -> topic-overlap guard fired.")
81	
82	        # (3) id<->idx offset: --task-name task72 with --idx 72 -> SystemExit (task72 is idx 56).
83	        raised = False
84	        try:
85	            _pack(72, task_name="task72", out_dir=td / "c3")
86	        except SystemExit as e:
87	            raised = "offset" in str(e).lower() or "resolves to" in str(e).lower() or "topic mismatch" in str(e).lower()
88	        if not raised:
89	            _fail("(3) --task-name task72 + --idx 72 did NOT raise the id<->idx offset SystemExit")
90	        print("(3) ok: --task-name task72 + --idx 72 -> id<->idx offset guard fired.")
91	
92	        # (4) correct pairing: --task-name task72 with --idx 56 -> clean pack.
93	        try:
94	            out = _pack(56, task_name="task72", out_dir=td / "c4")
95	        except SystemExit as e:
96	            _fail(f"(4) correct --task-name task72 + --idx 56 wrongly raised SystemExit: {e}")
97	        if not out.exists():
98	            _fail("(4) correct task-name+idx pairing did not write the packed file")
99	        print("(4) ok: --task-name task72 + --idx 56 packs cleanly.")
100	
101	    print(
102	        "PASS I-beatboth-011 idx15: the pack_drb2 topic-overlap guard fires on a wrong-idx mispairing "
103	        "(AI-labor report vs idx-72 Parkinson's) and the id<->idx offset guard catches --task-name "
104	        "task72 + --idx 72 (task72 is idx 56), while both correct pairings pack cleanly. "
105	        "§-1.4 silently-wrong-number closed."
106	    )
107	
108	
109	if __name__ == "__main__":
110	    main()

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""FAIL-LOUD behavioral harness for #1290 landmine-3 (TRACK termination-ROTATE).
3	
4	WHAT THIS PROVES (offline, no live LLM / no model load):
5	    On a total-deadline FORCE-CLOSE, the 4-role OpenRouter transport now ADDS the slow provider to
6	    `body["provider"]["ignore"]` BEFORE the bounded retry, so the NEXT POST targets a DIFFERENT host
7	    (the next entry in the pinned `order`). Before this fix, the retry rebuilt the SAME body and
8	    re-POSTed to the SAME slow provider (live evidence: 339 same-host 300s force-closes, 0 rotations).
9	
10	HOW (drives the REAL `OpenRouterRoleTransport.complete()` retry loop, not a re-implementation):
11	    * Monkeypatch the module-level `_post_with_total_deadline` so it raises
12	      `concurrent.futures.TimeoutError` for the first N attempts (each = a force-close that the REAL
13	      handler in `complete()` must rotate off), then returns a controlled success response on the
14	      next attempt. INSIDE the patched function we DEEP-COPY `body["provider"]["ignore"]` per attempt
15	      (it is mutated IN PLACE across the loop, so a live reference would alias the final list and make
16	      the growth assertion vacuous — the advisor's pitfall).
17	    * Monkeypatch `_build_openrouter_body` to a body with a KNOWN multi-provider `order` so the
18	      targeted-host inference is deterministic and offline (no routing-config dependency).
19	    * Neutralize the capture / cost side-effect modules so the success path completes without spend.
20	
21	ASSERTIONS (any failure -> sys.exit(1), FAIL LOUD):
22	    A. FORCE-CLOSE ROTATES: across the two simulated force-closes the captured per-attempt ignore
23	       sets GROW monotonically and never re-target an already-ignored host -> attempts 2 and 3 each
24	       point at a NEW provider (the grind is broken).
25	    B. SUCCESS PATH UNCHANGED: a clean run (zero force-close) leaves `ignore` byte-identical to the
26	       pinned baseline (no spurious rotation on the happy path).
27	    C. KILL-SWITCH: with PG_JUDGE_PROVIDER_ROTATE=0 a force-close does NOT rotate (the pre-fix
28	       byte-identical behavior is preserved for an operator opt-out).
29	
30	FAITHFULNESS: this harness only inspects WHICH provider the retry targets. It asserts nothing about
31	verdict parsing / role adjudication / span-grounding, none of which this fix touches.
32	"""
33	
34	from __future__ import annotations
35	
36	import concurrent.futures
37	import copy
38	import os
39	import sys
40	
41	# Repo root on sys.path so `src.*` imports resolve when run directly.
42	_REPO_ROOT = os.path.dirname(os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__)))
43	if _REPO_ROOT not in sys.path:
44	    sys.path.insert(0, _REPO_ROOT)
45	
46	import httpx  # noqa: E402
47	
48	from src.polaris_graph.roles import openrouter_role_transport as ort  # noqa: E402
49	from src.polaris_graph.roles.role_transport import RoleRequest  # noqa: E402
50	
51	
52	# A pinned multi-provider order so the targeted-host inference is deterministic and offline.
53	_PINNED_ORDER = ["z-ai", "baidu", "novita", "gmicloud"]
54	_BASE_URL = "https://openrouter.example/api/v1"
55	_MODEL_SLUG = "z-ai/glm-5.1"
56	
57	
58	class _FakeResponse:
59	    """Minimal stand-in for the httpx.Response the success path consumes."""
60	
61	    def __init__(self, payload: dict) -> None:
62	        self.status_code = httpx.codes.OK
63	        self.headers: dict = {}
64	        self._payload = payload
65	
66	    def json(self) -> dict:
67	        return self._payload
68	
69	
70	class _NoopCapture:
71	    """Stub for the pathB capture module (context-manager + no-op call sink)."""
72	
73	    def llm_role(self, _role):  # noqa: D401 - context manager
74	        class _Ctx:
75	            def __enter__(self_inner):
76	                return self_inner
77	
78	            def __exit__(self_inner, *exc):
79	                return False
80	
81	        return _Ctx()
82	
83	    def capture_llm_call(self, **_kwargs):
84	        return None
85	
86	
87	def _install_common_stubs(monkeypatch_targets: dict) -> None:
88	    """Patch endpoint resolution + body build + capture/cost side effects (offline)."""
89	    # Endpoint -> controlled tuple (base_url, api_key, model_slug).
90	    monkeypatch_targets["openrouter_role_endpoint"] = ort.openrouter_role_endpoint
91	    ort.openrouter_role_endpoint = lambda _role: (_BASE_URL, "sk-test", _MODEL_SLUG)  # type: ignore[assignment]
92	
93	    # Body build -> a body carrying the pinned provider order (fresh dict per call, like production).
94	    monkeypatch_targets["_build_openrouter_body"] = ort._build_openrouter_body
95	    ort._build_openrouter_body = (  # type: ignore[assignment]
96	        lambda _request, _model_slug, _msgs: {
97	            "model": _MODEL_SLUG,
98	            "messages": _msgs,
99	            "provider": {
100	                "order": list(_PINNED_ORDER),
101	                "ignore": [],
102	                "allow_fallbacks": False,
103	                "require_parameters": True,
104	            },
105	        }
106	    )
107	
108	    # Capture + verbatim-IO + cost orchestrator -> no-ops (no spend, no disk, no network).
109	    monkeypatch_targets["_pathb_capture"] = ort._pathb_capture
110	    ort._pathb_capture = _NoopCapture()  # type: ignore[assignment]
111	    monkeypatch_targets["_raw_io_capture"] = ort._raw_io_capture
112	    ort._raw_io_capture = lambda **_kwargs: None  # type: ignore[assignment]
113	
114	
115	def _restore(monkeypatch_targets: dict) -> None:
116	    for name, original in monkeypatch_targets.items():
117	        setattr(ort, name, original)
118	
119	
120	def _make_transport() -> ort.OpenRouterRoleTransport:
121	    # An httpx.Client is injected but never actually used (we patch _post_with_total_deadline).
122	    return ort.OpenRouterRoleTransport(httpx.Client())
123	
124	
125	def _success_payload() -> dict:
126	    return {
127	        "model": _MODEL_SLUG,
128	        "provider": "Z.AI",
129	        "choices": [{"message": {"content": "ENTAILED"}}],
130	        "usage": {"prompt_tokens": 10, "completion_tokens": 2},
131	    }
132	
133	
134	def _run_scenario(*, force_closes: int, rotate_env: str | None) -> list[list[str]]:
135	    """Drive the REAL complete() loop; return the per-POST-attempt deep-copied ignore snapshots.
136	
137	    `force_closes` = how many initial attempts raise concurrent.futures.TimeoutError (force-close);
138	    the next attempt returns the success payload. `rotate_env` sets PG_JUDGE_PROVIDER_ROTATE.
139	    """
140	    targets: dict = {}
141	    # Give the retry budget enough room for the requested number of force-closes + the success.
142	    prior_retries = os.environ.get("PG_ROLE_TRANSPORT_RETRIES")
143	    prior_rotate = os.environ.get("PG_JUDGE_PROVIDER_ROTATE")
144	    os.environ["PG_ROLE_TRANSPORT_RETRIES"] = str(force_closes + 1)
145	    if rotate_env is None:
146	        os.environ.pop("PG_JUDGE_PROVIDER_ROTATE", None)
147	    else:
148	        os.environ["PG_JUDGE_PROVIDER_ROTATE"] = rotate_env
149	
150	    snapshots: list[list[str]] = []
151	    attempts = {"n": 0}
152	
153	    def _fake_post(client, url, *, json_body, headers, timeout, total_s):
154	        # DEEP-COPY the ignore list NOW (it is mutated in place across the loop; a live ref aliases
155	        # the final list and makes the growth check vacuous).
156	        provider = json_body.get("provider") or {}
157	        snapshots.append(copy.deepcopy(provider.get("ignore", [])))
158	        attempts["n"] += 1
159	        if attempts["n"] <= force_closes:
160	            raise concurrent.futures.TimeoutError()
161	        return _FakeResponse(_success_payload())
162	
163	    try:
164	        _install_common_stubs(targets)
165	        targets["_post_with_total_deadline"] = ort._post_with_total_deadline
166	        ort._post_with_total_deadline = _fake_post  # type: ignore[assignment]
167	
168	        transport = _make_transport()
169	        req = RoleRequest(
170	            role="mirror",
171	            model_slug=_MODEL_SLUG,
172	            messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "verify"}],
173	            prompt="verify",
174	            params={},
175	        )
176	        resp = transport.complete(req)
177	        if getattr(resp, "raw_text", None) != "ENTAILED":
178	            raise AssertionError(
179	                f"success path did not return the expected verdict; got {resp!r}"
180	            )
181	    finally:
182	        _restore(targets)
183	        # restore env
184	        if prior_retries is None:
185	            os.environ.pop("PG_ROLE_TRANSPORT_RETRIES", None)
186	        else:
187	            os.environ["PG_ROLE_TRANSPORT_RETRIES"] = prior_retries
188	        if prior_rotate is None:
189	            os.environ.pop("PG_JUDGE_PROVIDER_ROTATE", None)
190	        else:
191	            os.environ["PG_JUDGE_PROVIDER_ROTATE"] = prior_rotate
192	
193	    return snapshots
194	
195	
196	def _fail(msg: str) -> None:
197	    print(f"FAIL: {msg}")
198	    sys.exit(1)
199	
200	
201	def main() -> int:
202	    print("=== #1290 landmine-3 provider-rotation behavioral harness ===")
203	
204	    # --- A. FORCE-CLOSE ROTATES (rotation default-ON) ---------------------------------------------
205	    # Two force-closes then a success: 3 POST attempts. The 1st targets z-ai (slow); rotation must
206	    # add z-ai to ignore so attempt 2 targets baidu; another force-close adds baidu so attempt 3
207	    # (success) targets novita.
208	    snaps = _run_scenario(force_closes=2, rotate_env=None)
209	    print(f"[A] default-ON, 2 force-closes: per-attempt ignore snapshots = {snaps}")
210	    if len(snaps) != 3:
211	        _fail(f"[A] expected 3 POST attempts (2 force-close + 1 success), got {len(snaps)}: {snaps}")
212	    # The ignore set must GROW monotonically (each force-close adds exactly one new host).
213	    set0, set1, set2 = set(snaps[0]), set(snaps[1]), set(snaps[2])
214	    if not (set0 == set() and set0 < set1 < set2):
215	        _fail(
216	            "[A] ignore set did NOT grow monotonically across force-closes "
217	            f"(expected {{}} < ... < ...); got {snaps}"
218	        )

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""I-beatboth-011 #1290 — fail-loud harness for the 4-role D8 seam TERMINATION fix.
3	
4	THE DEFECT (path-audit, verified live on a 3.8h grinding resume): a benchmark run could grind ~7.2h
5	instead of terminating at its advertised 3h run-wall, because:
6	  1. `_resolve_four_role_seam_timeout()` defaults to `max(7200, 4*6500)=26000s` (~7.22h) and
7	     `PG_FOUR_ROLE_SEAM_TIMEOUT_SECONDS` was pinned ONLY in `_SMOKE_SCALE_OVERRIDES`, so a full/--resume
8	     run used the 7.2h default.
9	  2. the seam is awaited via a BLOCKING `.result(timeout=_seam_timeout)` on the event-loop thread, which
10	     starves the enclosing `asyncio.wait_for(run_one_query, run-wall)` — so the run-wall never fires.
11	  3. preflight checked only generator<section<run-wall, NOT seam<=run-wall — so it passed while the seam
12	     could exceed the wall.
13	
14	THE FIX (timeout plumbing only — faithfulness engine UNTOUCHED):
15	  A. `_FULL_CAPABILITY_BENCHMARK_SLATE` now pins `PG_FOUR_ROLE_SEAM_TIMEOUT_SECONDS=7200` (< run-wall) and
16	     it rides `_BENCHMARK_FORCE_EXACT_FLAGS` so a stale .env cannot restore the grind.
17	  B. the seam `.result()` timeout is capped by the REMAINING run-wall budget
18	     (`min(_seam_timeout, deadline-now)`), so the blocking call can never overrun the wall.
19	  C. `preflight_full_capability` fail-CLOSES when the resolved seam timeout EXCEEDS the run-wall.
20	
21	This harness asserts A/B/C OFFLINE (no live LLM / model load). FAIL LOUD (non-zero exit) on regression.
22	"""
23	from __future__ import annotations
24	
25	import os
26	import re
27	import sys
28	import time
29	from pathlib import Path
30	
31	_REPO = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
32	sys.path.insert(0, str(_REPO))
33	
34	
35	def _fail(msg: str) -> None:
36	    print(f"FAIL I-beatboth-011 #1290 seam-termination: {msg}")
37	    sys.exit(1)
38	
39	
40	def main() -> None:
41	    import scripts.dr_benchmark.run_gate_b as rgb
42	    from scripts.run_honest_sweep_r3 import (
43	        _resolve_four_role_seam_timeout as _resolve_seam,
44	        run_wall_clock_seconds,
45	    )
46	
47	    # (A) the FULL slate bounds the seam below the run-wall + force-exact ------------------------------
48	    slate = rgb._FULL_CAPABILITY_BENCHMARK_SLATE
49	    if "PG_FOUR_ROLE_SEAM_TIMEOUT_SECONDS" not in slate:
50	        _fail("(A) the FULL slate does NOT pin PG_FOUR_ROLE_SEAM_TIMEOUT_SECONDS (the 7.2h grind survives)")
51	    seam_slate = int(slate["PG_FOUR_ROLE_SEAM_TIMEOUT_SECONDS"])
52	    wall_slate = int(slate["PG_RUN_WALL_CLOCK_SEC"])
53	    if not (seam_slate <= wall_slate):
54	        _fail(f"(A) slate seam {seam_slate} NOT <= run-wall {wall_slate} — seam can grind past the wall")
55	    if "PG_FOUR_ROLE_SEAM_TIMEOUT_SECONDS" not in rgb._BENCHMARK_FORCE_EXACT_FLAGS:
56	        _fail("(A) PG_FOUR_ROLE_SEAM_TIMEOUT_SECONDS is NOT force-exact — a stale .env could restore the grind")
57	    print(f"(A) ok: full slate pins seam={seam_slate}s <= run-wall={wall_slate}s, force-exact.")
58	
59	    # (B) resolver: the env WINS OUTRIGHT (so the slate pin actually lowers it); unset => the grind -----
60	    _saved = os.environ.get("PG_FOUR_ROLE_SEAM_TIMEOUT_SECONDS")
61	    try:
62	        os.environ["PG_FOUR_ROLE_SEAM_TIMEOUT_SECONDS"] = "5400"
63	        if _resolve_seam() != 5400.0:
64	            _fail(f"(B) env override ignored: _resolve_four_role_seam_timeout()={_resolve_seam()} != 5400")
65	        os.environ.pop("PG_FOUR_ROLE_SEAM_TIMEOUT_SECONDS", None)
66	        default_seam = _resolve_seam()
67	        if default_seam <= wall_slate:
68	            print(f"(B) note: unset default seam={default_seam:.0f}s (<= wall only because gen timeout small)")
69	        else:
70	            print(f"(B) confirmed: unset default seam={default_seam:.0f}s EXCEEDS wall {wall_slate} "
71	                  f"(this is the grind the slate pin + force-exact prevent)")
72	    finally:
73	        if _saved is None:
74	            os.environ.pop("PG_FOUR_ROLE_SEAM_TIMEOUT_SECONDS", None)
75	        else:
76	            os.environ["PG_FOUR_ROLE_SEAM_TIMEOUT_SECONDS"] = _saved
77	    print("(B) ok: PG_FOUR_ROLE_SEAM_TIMEOUT_SECONDS wins outright in the resolver.")
78	
79	    # (C) the preflight seam<=run-wall condition: holds at the slate values, violated when seam>wall -----
80	    _saved_seam = os.environ.get("PG_FOUR_ROLE_SEAM_TIMEOUT_SECONDS")
81	    _saved_wall = os.environ.get("PG_RUN_WALL_CLOCK_SEC")
82	    try:
83	        os.environ["PG_RUN_WALL_CLOCK_SEC"] = "10800"
84	        os.environ["PG_FOUR_ROLE_SEAM_TIMEOUT_SECONDS"] = "7200"
85	        if not (float(_resolve_seam()) <= float(run_wall_clock_seconds())):
86	            _fail("(C) the slate-pinned seam should satisfy seam<=run-wall but does not")
87	        os.environ["PG_FOUR_ROLE_SEAM_TIMEOUT_SECONDS"] = "99999"
88	        if float(_resolve_seam()) <= float(run_wall_clock_seconds()):
89	            _fail("(C) a seam (99999) ABOVE the wall (10800) did NOT violate seam<=run-wall — preflight blind")
90	    finally:
91	        for k, v in (("PG_FOUR_ROLE_SEAM_TIMEOUT_SECONDS", _saved_seam), ("PG_RUN_WALL_CLOCK_SEC", _saved_wall)):
92	            if v is None:
93	                os.environ.pop(k, None)
94	            else:
95	                os.environ[k] = v
96	    # verify the preflight actually CARRIES the seam<=run-wall fail-closed assertion (source-anchored)
97	    pf_src = Path(_REPO / "scripts" / "dr_benchmark" / "run_gate_b.py").read_text(encoding="utf-8", errors="replace")
98	    if "seam timeout" not in pf_src or "EXCEEDS the run-wall" not in pf_src:
99	        _fail("(C) preflight_full_capability is MISSING the seam<=run-wall fail-closed assertion")
100	    print("(C) ok: preflight fail-closes when the resolved seam timeout exceeds the run-wall.")
101	
102	    # (D) the seam .result() is capped by the remaining run-wall budget --------------------------------
103	    # (D1) the cap formula: with a near deadline, the effective wait is bounded; with no deadline, unchanged.
104	    seam_timeout = 7200.0
105	    deadline_near = time.monotonic() + 90.0
106	    eff_capped = max(1.0, min(float(seam_timeout), deadline_near - time.monotonic()))
107	    if not (eff_capped <= 95.0):
108	        _fail(f"(D1) cap formula did not bound the wait to the remaining wall (~90s): got {eff_capped:.1f}s")
109	    eff_uncapped = float(seam_timeout)  # deadline is None -> unchanged behaviour
110	    if eff_uncapped != 7200.0:
111	        _fail("(D1) with no deadline the effective timeout must equal the resolved seam timeout (unchanged)")
112	    # (D2) the cap wiring is PRESENT at the seam call site (regression guard for the blocking-.result fix)
113	    sweep_src = Path(_REPO / "scripts" / "run_honest_sweep_r3.py").read_text(encoding="utf-8", errors="replace")
114	    needed = ["_seam_effective_timeout", "_RUN_WALL_CLOCK_DEADLINE_CTX.get()", "min(float(_seam_timeout)"]
115	    missing = [n for n in needed if n not in sweep_src]
116	    if missing:
117	        _fail(f"(D2) the seam .result() run-wall cap wiring is MISSING from run_honest_sweep_r3.py: {missing}")
118	    if re.search(r"\.result\(\s*\n?\s*timeout=_seam_timeout\s*\n?\s*\)", sweep_src):
119	        _fail("(D2) the seam still calls .result(timeout=_seam_timeout) UNCAPPED — the blocking-overrun fix regressed")
120	    print("(D) ok: the seam .result() is capped by the remaining run-wall budget (formula + wiring present).")
121	
122	    print(
123	        "PASS I-beatboth-011 #1290: the 4-role D8 seam is BOUNDED in the full slate (force-exact) below the "
124	        "run-wall (A), the env wins outright so the pin takes (B), preflight fail-closes on seam>run-wall (C), "
125	        "and the seam .result() is capped by the remaining run-wall budget so the blocking call cannot overrun "
126	        "the wall (D). A full/--resume run now terminates inside its wall. Faithfulness engine untouched."
127	    )
128	
129	
130	if __name__ == "__main__":
131	    main()

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""FORCED-STALL teardown proof that the W6 outer wall-deadline ABANDONS an UNCANCELLABLE basket task
3	and that ``asyncio.run`` then completes with ZERO pending tasks (no shutdown hang), instead of wedging
4	the whole process at interpreter teardown.
5	
6	WHY AN UNCANCELLABLE WEDGE (I-wire-001 W6 #1314): a plain ``await asyncio.sleep(30)`` is CANCELLABLE —
7	a bare ``t.cancel()`` tears it down, so it does NOT exercise the force-close path and is NOT a real
8	regression guard. The proven hang class is a task whose cancellation is SWALLOWED (httpx client teardown
9	that re-blocks, or any ``except CancelledError`` that awaits again). ``asyncio.run``'s shutdown calls
10	``_cancel_all_tasks`` which ``gather``-awaits EVERY still-pending task BEFORE ``loop.close()`` — so one
11	uncancellable task hangs the process at exit. This proof's stub catches CancelledError in the stuck
12	basket and RE-BLOCKS in a ``finally`` (await again) — so bare ``t.cancel()`` is provably insufficient.
13	The fix under test is the ported access_bypass detach/force-close (``_coro.close()`` raises GeneratorExit,
14	finalizing the task synchronously) applied at abandon time, which excludes the task from the await-list.
15	
16	PROOF (mechanical, the one that failed before): "zero pending at shutdown" is NOT queryable after
17	``asyncio.run`` returns (the loop is closed). So we run ``asyncio.run(abstractive_pre_pass(...))`` in a
18	DAEMON THREAD and ``join(timeout)``: if the thread is still alive after the timeout, ``asyncio.run`` HUNG
19	at ``_cancel_all_tasks`` awaiting the uncancellable task -> FAIL LOUD. We ALSO assert the module-level
20	``_DETACHED_WRITER_TASKS`` set drained empty (the force-close discarded the finalized task).
21	
22	Asserts: (1) ``asyncio.run`` COMPLETES (thread joins) well under the timeout -> zero wedged pending at
23	shutdown; (2) the detached-task set is EMPTY after teardown; (3) the stuck basket is ABSENT from out
24	(-> K-span fallback); (4) >=1 healthy basket IS drafted (abandon didn't kill healthy work).
25	
26	No real API key needed — the writer is monkeypatched.
27	"""
28	from __future__ import annotations
29	
30	import asyncio
31	import json
32	import os
33	import sys
34	import threading
35	import time
36	from pathlib import Path
37	
38	_REPO = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
39	sys.path.insert(0, str(_REPO))
40	
41	os.environ["PG_ABSTRACTIVE_WRITER"] = "1"
42	os.environ["PG_STRICT_VERIFY_ENTAILMENT"] = "enforce"
43	os.environ.setdefault("PG_GENERATOR_MODEL", "z-ai/glm-5.2")
44	os.environ["PG_ABSTRACTIVE_WRITER_CONCURRENCY"] = "4"
45	os.environ["PG_ABSTRACTIVE_WRITER_CALL_DEADLINE_S"] = "60"  # ABOVE the wall -> per-call deadline does NOT fire
46	os.environ["PG_ABSTRACTIVE_WRITER_WALL_DEADLINE_S"] = "8"   # the binding bound under test
47	os.environ["PG_ABSTRACTIVE_WRITER_MAX_RETRIES"] = "0"
48	
49	_GOLD = _REPO / "tests" / "fixtures" / "iwire001" / "compose_gold_corrected.json"
50	WALL = 8.0
51	# Generous: wall(8) + abandon force-close + asyncio.run shutdown. If asyncio.run instead HANGS on the
52	# uncancellable task, the thread stays alive PAST this and join() returns with is_alive()=True -> FAIL.
53	JOIN_TIMEOUT_S = 40.0
54	
55	
56	def _materialize(gold, n):
57	    from src.polaris_graph.synthesis.credibility_pass import (
58	        BasketMember, ClaimBasket, MEMBER_TIER_ENTAILMENT_VERIFIED,
59	    )
60	
61	    def _member(eid, span, tier, weight):
62	        return BasketMember(
63	            evidence_id=eid, source_url=f"https://corpus/{eid}", source_tier=tier or "T1",
64	            origin_cluster_id=f"o::{eid}", credibility_weight=weight, authority_score=weight,
65	            span=(0, len(span)), direct_quote=span, span_verdict="SUPPORTS",
66	            member_tier=MEMBER_TIER_ENTAILMENT_VERIFIED,
67	        )
68	
69	    pool = dict(gold["evidence_pool"])
70	    out = []
71	    for b in gold["baskets"][:n]:
72	        members = [_member(m["eid"], m["span"], m["tier"], m["weight"]) for m in b["members"]]
73	        out.append(ClaimBasket(
74	            claim_cluster_id=b["claim_cluster_id"], claim_text=b["claim_text"],
75	            subject=b["subject"], predicate="finding", supporting_members=members,
76	            refuter_cluster_ids=(), weight_mass=float(len(members)),
77	            total_clustered_origin_count=len(members),
78	            verified_support_origin_count=len(members), basket_verdict="full",
79	        ))
80	    return out, pool
81	
82	
83	def main():
84	    gold = json.loads(_GOLD.read_text(encoding="utf-8", errors="replace"))
85	    from src.polaris_graph.generator import abstractive_writer as aw
86	    from src.polaris_graph.generator.provenance_generator import verify_sentence_provenance
87	    from src.polaris_graph.generator.verified_compose import build_verified_span_draft
88	
89	    baskets, pool = _materialize(gold, 3)
90	    stuck_key = aw._basket_key(baskets[0])
91	    stuck_eid = str(getattr(baskets[0].supporting_members[0], "evidence_id", ""))
92	    healthy_keys = [aw._basket_key(b) for b in baskets[1:]]
93	
94	    async def _uncancellable_wedge() -> None:
95	        # The proven hang class: cancellation is SWALLOWED and the frame RE-BLOCKS. A bare t.cancel()
96	        # cannot finalize this — only _coro.close() (GeneratorExit) can. shield() keeps the inner await
97	        # alive across the cancel; the finally re-blocks so even the post-shield CancelledError can't end it.
98	        try:
99	            await asyncio.shield(asyncio.sleep(3600))
100	        except asyncio.CancelledError:
101	            # Re-block: swallow the cancel and await again, exactly like an httpx teardown that wedges.
102	            try:
103	                await asyncio.sleep(3600)
104	            except asyncio.CancelledError:
105	                await asyncio.sleep(3600)  # and again — uncancellable by design
106	        return None
107	
108	    async def _stub(members, evidence_pool, **kw):
109	        eid = str(getattr(members[0], "evidence_id", ""))
110	        if eid == stuck_eid:
111	            await _uncancellable_wedge()   # UNCANCELLABLE stall (swallows CancelledError, re-blocks)
112	            return ""
113	        for b in baskets[1:]:              # healthy baskets return their real verbatim K-span draft
114	            if str(getattr(b.supporting_members[0], "evidence_id", "")) == eid:
115	                return build_verified_span_draft(b, evidence_pool) or ""
116	        return ""
117	
118	    orig = aw._call_writer
119	    aw._call_writer = _stub
120	    aw._DETACHED_WRITER_TASKS.clear()
121	    writer_verify = aw.make_writer_verify_fn(verify_sentence_provenance)
122	
123	    result: dict = {}
124	
125	    def _run_in_thread() -> None:
126	        # asyncio.run on a fresh loop in this thread: if _cancel_all_tasks at shutdown awaits the
127	        # uncancellable task, THIS thread never returns and join() below will report is_alive()=True.
128	        result["out"] = asyncio.run(
129	            aw.abstractive_pre_pass(baskets, pool, writer_verify_fn=writer_verify)
130	        )
131	
132	    th = threading.Thread(target=_run_in_thread, name="prepass_asyncio_run", daemon=True)
133	    t0 = time.time()
134	    th.start()
135	    th.join(JOIN_TIMEOUT_S)
136	    dt = time.time() - t0
137	    aw._call_writer = orig
138	
139	    print(f"[prove_teardown] asyncio.run thread alive_after_join={th.is_alive()} in {dt:.1f}s", flush=True)
140	    ok = True
141	    if th.is_alive():
142	        print(f"FAIL: asyncio.run HUNG at shutdown ({dt:.1f}s >= {JOIN_TIMEOUT_S}s join timeout) — the "
143	              f"uncancellable task wedged _cancel_all_tasks (force-close did NOT finalize it)", flush=True)
144	        # The process itself will not exit cleanly because of the daemon thread's wedged loop; fail loud.
145	        print("FAIL prove_teardown", flush=True)
146	        os._exit(1)
147	    out = result.get("out", {})
148	    remaining = len(aw._DETACHED_WRITER_TASKS)
149	    if remaining != 0:
150	        print(f"FAIL: {remaining} detached task(s) NOT drained after teardown (force-close/discard leak)", flush=True); ok = False
151	    if stuck_key in out:
152	        print(f"FAIL: stuck basket {stuck_key} should be ABSENT (abandoned), but is in out", flush=True); ok = False
153	    if not any(k in out for k in healthy_keys):
154	        print(f"FAIL: no healthy basket drafted; healthy_keys={healthy_keys}", flush=True); ok = False
155	    print(("PASS prove_teardown: asyncio.run COMPLETED with ZERO pending at shutdown despite an "
156	           "UNCANCELLABLE wedged basket (force-close finalized it); detached set drained empty; stuck "
157	           "basket ABANDONED to K-span; healthy baskets drafted.") if ok else "FAIL prove_teardown", flush=True)
158	    sys.exit(0 if ok else 1)
159	
160	
161	if __name__ == "__main__":
162	    main()
